title: Foundations to Advanced Synthesis
concept: Tying it all together: word vectors and RNNs provided the sequence background; multi-head attention and scaling enabled the Transformer revolution.

exercises.prompt: Which two concepts served as the foundation for modern language models?
exercises.explain: Word vectors represent the meaning of individual words, while sequence models (like RNNs) help track the order and flow of words in sentences.

cards.title: The AI Super-Brain
cards.body: Have you ever wondered how tools like ChatGPT can understand long paragraphs and write back instantly? It all started when scientists combined word vectors and sequence models into a massive breakthrough: the Transformer.

cards.title: What is a Transformer?
cards.body: The Transformer is a powerful AI design that processes entire sequences of words at the same time. By using self-attention, it immediately calculates how much every word in a sentence relates to every other word, no matter how far apart they are.

cards.title: The Translator Team
cards.body:
Imagine trying to translate a book. An RNN is like a translator who reads one word at a time, sometimes forgetting the beginning by the time they reach the end of a long sentence. 

A Transformer is like a team of translators looking at the entire page all at once, highlighting how words relate to each other instantly.

cards.title: Context in Action
cards.body:
In the sentence, 'The bank of the river had a bank building next to it,' an older model might get confused by the word 'bank'. 

A Transformer looks at 'river' and 'building' at the same time to understand that the first 'bank' is land, and the second 'bank' is a financial office.

cards.title: Not Just a Bigger RNN
cards.body: Many people think Transformers are just bigger version of Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs). In reality, Transformers abandoned the step-by-step nature of RNNs entirely. This shift allowed them to train on massive amounts of data much faster.

cards.title: RNN vs. Transformer
cards.body: See how the processing style changed to allow for faster training and better understanding of long texts.

left.text: Processes word-by-word. Slow on long sentences, easily forgets the beginning.

right.text: Processes all words at once. Connects any two words instantly.

exercises.prompt: What was a major limitation of older sequence models like RNNs?

options.text: They processed words in parallel, all at the same time.
They processed words one by one, making them slow to train on long texts.
They did not use word vectors at all.
They were too simple to run on modern computers.

exercises.explain: Recurrent Neural Networks process words sequentially (one after another), which makes them slow to train on huge datasets compared to parallel-processing Transformers.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Transformers must read a sentence from left to right, one word at a time, before they can start understanding it.
exercises.explain: Transformers process all tokens or words simultaneously, which is why they scale so well on modern hardware.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to complete the description of how Transformers link words together.
exercises.sentence: Transformers use self-___ to link words in a sentence regardless of their distance.
exercises.explain: Self-attention allows the model to look at all parts of a sentence simultaneously to figure out how words relate to one another.

exercises.prompt: Which of these represents a modern Transformer AI using self-attention?

options.text: An AI model that instantly connects the word 'it' to 'the laptop' because they both appear in the same paragraph.
A basic program that highlights every vowel in a text document.
A system that randomly shuffles the order of words in a sentence.

exercises.explain: Only Option A shows an AI using self-attention to correctly resolve the meaning of a pronoun ('it') based on context clues. Option B is a simple search, and Option C is just random text generation.

exercises.prompt: Why did Transformers successfully replace RNNs for understanding long pieces of text?

options.text: They process words one by one, making them slower but more accurate than RNNs.
They process all words at once and use self-attention to find connections across the whole text.
They rely purely on hand-written rules instead of training on large datasets.
They completely discard word embeddings and only look at character counts.

exercises.explain: Transformers process all words in a sequence simultaneously (parallel processing) and use self-attention to calculate how words relate to one another, bypassing the step-by-step limits of older RNN designs.
